questions.question: Who became Rome's first emperor in 27 BC after defeating Mark Antony and Cleopatra at Actium in 31 BC?
questions.explanation: Gaius Octavius (Augustus), Julius Caesar's adopted son, defeated Mark Antony and Cleopatra at Actium. The Senate granted him the title 'Augustus' in 27 BC. He ruled 41 years. The month of August is named after him.
questions.memory_hook: Augustus (Octavian): won at Actium 31 BC. First emperor 27 BC. August named for him. Caesar's adopted son.

questions.question: What do the kidneys do, and how much blood do they filter per day?
options: Filter waste from blood and produce urine; about 200 liters/day
Filter blood and produce bile; about 200 liters/day
questions.answer: Filter waste from blood and produce urine; about 200 liters/day
questions.explanation: The kidneys filter ~200 liters of blood daily, producing 1-2 liters of urine. They regulate blood pressure, pH, and electrolytes. Each kidney has ~1 million nephrons. Dialysis replaces kidney function when they fail.
questions.memory_hook: Kidneys: filter 200 liters/day, 1 million nephrons each, 1-2 liters urine produced.

questions.question: In what order are the four Grand Slam tennis tournaments played each year?
options: Australian Open (Jan), French Open (May-Jun), Wimbledon (Jun-Jul), US Open (Aug-Sep)
Wimbledon, US Open, Australian Open, French Open
French Open, Wimbledon, US Open, Australian Open
Australian Open, Wimbledon, French Open, US Open
questions.answer: Australian Open (Jan), French Open (May-Jun), Wimbledon (Jun-Jul), US Open (Aug-Sep)
questions.explanation: The Grand Slams in calendar order: Australian Open (January, hard), French Open (May-June, clay), Wimbledon (June-July, grass), US Open (August-September, hard). Winning all four in one year is the 'Grand Slam' — done by Steffi Graf (1988) and Rod Laver (1962, 1969).
questions.memory_hook: Grand Slam order: Australian (Jan) → French (May-Jun) → Wimbledon (Jun-Jul) → US Open (Aug-Sep).

questions.question: Which Studio Ghibli director made 'Spirited Away' (2001) — Japan's highest-grossing film and an Academy Award winner?
questions.explanation: Miyazaki co-founded Studio Ghibli in 1985. Spirited Away (2001) won the Best Animated Feature Oscar and is Japan's highest-grossing film. He was awarded an Honorary Oscar in 2014. Has announced retirement multiple times.
questions.memory_hook: Miyazaki: Studio Ghibli 1985. Spirited Away = Japan's biggest film + Oscar. Has 'retired' multiple times.

questions.question: Where does the Nile Delta — where the Nile meets the Mediterranean — sit?
questions.explanation: The Nile Delta fans across northern Egypt — approximately 240 km of Mediterranean coastline and 22,000 km² of fertile land. The Aswan High Dam now blocks the sediment that built and sustained the delta.
questions.memory_hook: Nile Delta: northern Egypt. 240 km of coast. Aswan Dam blocks the sediment that once built it.

questions.question: John Calvin established a theocracy in which Swiss city from 1541, influencing the Scottish Kirk, Dutch Reformed Church, and American Puritanism?
questions.explanation: Calvin established a theocratic city-state in Geneva from 1541 until his death in 1564. His theology stressed predestination and Bible authority. It influenced Calvinist churches including the Scottish Kirk, Dutch Reformed Church, and American Puritan churches.
questions.memory_hook: Calvin: Geneva theocracy 1541. Predestination, Bible authority. Influenced Scottish, Dutch, American Puritan traditions.

questions.question: Which actor won consecutive Best Actor Oscars (1993 and 1994) and is known for roles in 'Forrest Gump' and 'Cast Away'?
questions.explanation: Tom Hanks won for 'Philadelphia' (1993) and 'Forrest Gump' (1994), becoming only the 2nd actor (after Spencer Tracy) to win in consecutive years.
questions.memory_hook: Tom Hanks: consecutive Oscars 1993-94 (Philadelphia + Forrest Gump). 2nd actor after Spencer Tracy to do it. 'Wilson!'

questions.question: Which part of a neuron carries electrical signals away from the cell body at speeds up to 120 m/s?
questions.explanation: Axons carry signals away; dendrites receive them. Myelin insulates axons, allowing speeds up to 120 m/s. The longest human axon runs from the base of the spine to the big toe (~1 meter). Synapses are gaps between neurons.
questions.memory_hook: Axon: signal away from neuron. Myelin = insulation, up to 120 m/s. Longest axon = 1 meter (spine to toe).

questions.question: The Pacific Ocean is the Earth's largest ocean. How does it compare in size to all of Earth's land masses combined?
options: Roughly equal to all land combined
About half the size of all land combined
Twice the size of all land combined
questions.explanation: The Pacific Ocean (165 million km²) is larger than all of Earth's land masses combined (~150 million km²). It contains the Mariana Trench (deepest point), the Ring of Fire, and the Great Pacific Garbage Patch.
questions.memory_hook: Pacific: 165M km² — bigger than all land on Earth combined. Contains Mariana Trench and Ring of Fire.

questions.question: Which physicist, who explained nuclear fission but was overlooked for the Nobel Prize, had element 109 (meitnerium) named after her?
questions.explanation: Lise Meitner explained nuclear fission in 1939 but was not included in the Nobel Prize given to Otto Hahn alone. She had fled Nazi Germany as a Jewish woman in 1938. Element 109, meitnerium, is named after her.
questions.memory_hook: Lise Meitner: explained fission, denied Nobel (Hahn won). Jewish refugee from Nazis. Element 109 = meitnerium.

questions.question: When did the Rolling Stones actually form, and what was their signature lips-and-tongue logo first used?
options: Formed 1962; logo designed 1971 by John Pasche
questions.answer: Formed 1962; logo designed 1971 by John Pasche
questions.explanation: The Rolling Stones formed in 1962 when Jagger and Richards renewed their childhood friendship on a train. The famous lips and tongue logo was designed by John Pasche in 1971. Their 'A Bigger Bang' Tour (2005-07) grossed $558M — highest-grossing rock tour.
questions.memory_hook: Stones: formed 1962, Jagger + Richards met on train. Lips logo designed 1971 by Pasche. $558M tour record.

questions.question: The Boston Celtics and Los Angeles Lakers both have 17 NBA championships each — what happened in 2024?
options: Celtics won their 18th, taking sole possession of the record
They tied again in the Finals
questions.answer: Celtics won their 18th, taking sole possession of the record
questions.explanation: The Boston Celtics won their 18th NBA championship in 2024, breaking the tie with the Lakers and becoming the franchise with the most NBA titles outright. The Celtics had previously dominated the 1950s-60s with Bill Russell.
questions.memory_hook: Celtics: won 18th NBA title in 2024, breaking tie with Lakers. Most championships in NBA history.
